title: notifications-push — field classification
description: device_token is RESTRICTED (encrypted, never returned). Every other field is public or internal. Push notification content itself is CONFIDENTIAL (may include recipient name / activity details).

regulated_minor: note: When PushSubscription.user_id refers to a minor recipient (consent_age_gate=minor_under_consent_age on the User model), the entire row inherits regulated_minor tier — reduced retention window (30d vs 90d), tighter COPPA gates, cascade-delete on VPC withdrawal.
audit_log: audits.notifications-push.* rows for subscription create + revoke + expire events. device_token NEVER audit-logged — fingerprint only.

notes: device_token is the highest-sensitivity field in this module. AES-256 encrypted at rest via AWS KMS. Never returned in any API response (hidden array + explicit exclusion). Only decrypted server-side during SendPushJob for the provider call.
device_token_fingerprint is SHA-256(device_token). Non-secret — used for admin listings + support diagnostics + duplicate-detection on register.

The push notification payload (alert.title + alert.body + custom_data) is CONFIDENTIAL because it may include recipient's own name, activity summaries, etc. Delivered to OS-owned notification storage which we cannot audit.
